_meta.description: Feasibility of modeling the millennial swing as a hallstatt×jose4-driven component: route 5C (5th constant-amplitude stack cycle, phase-locked, modern-window amplitude ceiling) vs route 4F (4th force: guard-constrained transient resonator + capped drive tone).

verdict: Route 5C is architecturally dead: the modern window caps a constant 1,014-yr tone at 19 s (free fit wants 59 s), absorbing only 2 s of RMS — a 5th stack cycle cannot carry the swing. Route 4F is FEASIBLE: a guard-passing transient resonator (T₀ = 4000 yr, Q = 0.55) + capped drive reaches RMS 119 s (vs 52.7 unconstrained, 269 raw) while staying quiet in the modern window and at J2000. The 4th-force architecture (transient, decaying) is the only viable implementation — as physics predicted. Shipping would require: an envelope/ring-down formalism in the runtime (new architecture), an agreed excitation epoch, and the anchor-closure design. Driving attribution to hallstatt×jose4 remains marginal (n = 2) — hold as-if status.
